2026-04-17T08:56:39.1396030Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1397026Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1398088Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1399047Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1399619Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1400056Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1400342Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1400711Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1401022Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1401434Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1401790Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1402293Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:56:39.1402761Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T09:01:06.7858462Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T09:00:46.659503241Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #58149 (commit `91e692166d`) by @live1206\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58149 completed the MPG migration for `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric`, regenerating the entire package from TypeSpec. This introduced/confirmed all core resource types (`ServiceFabricClusterResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`, `ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`, `ServiceFabricServiceResource`, `ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`) and their associated collections and extension methods.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG is empty for 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)**: The TypeSpec migration should be documented — at minimum noting the regeneration and any behavioral changes or new API surface introduced by the 2023-11-01-preview API version.\n- **README \"Key concepts\" section is generic**: It only links to the general Azure SDK docs page. There are no ServiceFabric-specific key concepts (clusters, node types, applications, application types, services, reliability/durability levels, upgrade policies, etc.).\n- **README \"Examples\" section has no inline code snippets**: It only links to a general samples gallery. There are no embedded code examples for the most common operations (create a cluster, list clusters by subscription, create an application, update a cluster).\n- **No handwritten sample files with `#region Snippet:` tags**: Generated samples exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but lack `#region Snippet:` markers and are not wired to README placeholders, so the Update-Snippets tooling cannot inject them into the README.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Update the CHANGELOG\n\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/CHANGELOG.md`\n**Action:** Edit — populate the `1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)` section.\n\n```markdown\n## 1.2.0-beta.3 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Migrated SDK generation from AutoRest to TypeSpec.\n- Regenerated from the `2023-11-01-preview` API version specification.\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Update README Key Concepts section\n\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md`\n**Action:** Edit — replace the current \"Key concepts\" section body with ServiceFabric-specific concepts.\n\nReplace:\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).\n```\n\nWith:\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric` library provides management-plane access to Azure Service Fabric resources. The key resource hierarchy is:\n\n- **`ServiceFabricClusterResource`** — represents a Service Fabric cluster; this is the top-level resource managed by this library. Clusters define the node types, reliability level, and durability level for the distributed system.\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeResource`** — represents a named application type registered on the cluster.\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationTypeVersionResource`** — represents a specific version of an application type, including the package URL.\n- **`ServiceFabricApplicationResource`** — represents a deployed instance of an application type on a cluster.\n- **`ServiceFabricServiceResource`** — represents a service within a deployed application.\n- **`ServiceFabricVmSizeResource`** — read-only resource exposing the supported VM sizes for Service Fabric node types in a given location.\n\nFor additional general concepts about the Azure Resource Manager .NET SDK, see the [Azure SDK for .NET documentation](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html).\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 3: Add handwritten sample file with snippet regions\n\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/tests/Samples/Sample_ServiceFabricCluster.cs` *(create)*\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Core;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Models;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample_ServiceFabricCluster\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task CreateOrUpdateCluster()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId);\n\n            ServiceFabricClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroupResource.GetServiceFabricClusters();\n\n            ServiceFabricClusterData data = new ServiceFabricClusterData(new AzureLocation(\"eastus\"))\n            {\n                ManagementEndpoint = new Uri(\"https://myCluster.eastus.cloudapp.azure.com:19080\"),\n                NodeTypes =\n                {\n                    new ClusterNodeTypeDescription(\"nt1vm\", 19000, 19007, true, 5)\n                    {\n                        ApplicationPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(20000, 30000),\n                        EphemeralPorts = new ClusterEndpointRangeDescription(49000, 64000),\n                        DurabilityLevel = ClusterDurabilityLevel.Silver,\n                    }\n                },\n                ReliabilityLevel = ClusterReliabilityLevel.Silver,\n                FabricSettings =\n                {\n                    new SettingsSectionDescription(\"Security\", new[]\n                    {\n                        new SettingsParameterDescription(\"ClusterProtectionLevel\", \"EncryptAndSign\")\n                    })\n                },\n            };\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cServiceFabricClusterResource\u003e lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \"myCluster\", data);\n            ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Cluster ID: {cluster.Id}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task ListClustersBySubscription()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\n\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\n\n            await foreach (ServiceFabricClusterResource cluster in subscription.GetServiceFabricClustersAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Cluster: {cluster.Data.Name}, Location: {cluster.Data.Location}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 4: Update README Examples section with snippet placeholders\n\n**File:** `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md`\n**Action:** Edit — replace the current \"Examples\" section body.\n\nReplace:\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples)\n```\n\nWith:\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Service Fabric cluster\n\n```C# Snippet:CreateServiceFabricCluster\n```\n\n### List all clusters in a subscription\n\n```C# Snippet:ListServiceFabricClusters\n```\n\nFor additional code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples).\n```\n\n\u003e **Note:** The closing fences above are triple-backtick fences in the actual README. They are shown here inside a code block for illustration. Ensure the snippet placeholder fences use exactly `` ```C# Snippet:\u003cName\u003e `` and `` ``` `` on the closing line.\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/README.md` already contains all required top-level sections in the correct order. The following sections need content improvements (addressed above):\n\n| Section | Status |\n|---|---|\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete |\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete |\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Complete |\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only — add ServiceFabric-specific concepts (Step 2 above) |\n| Examples | ⚠️ Generic link only — add inline snippets (Step 4 above) |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete |\n| Next steps | ✅ Complete |\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete |\n\n---\n\n### Step 6: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicefabric` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into README.md placeholders; check git diff to confirm README was updated\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicefabric` — regenerates API listing files (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm)\n5. `dotnet format sdk/servicefabric/Azure.ResourceManager.ServiceFabric/` — ensures consistent code formatting\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[ServiceFabric] Docs: README examples, key concepts, and CHANGELOG missing after TypeSpec migration"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
